candidates.title: 'Let your Yes be Yes and your No, No' (Mt 5:37) IS the bit -- the law of excluded middle, no third value

Christ forbids the graded oath-ladder (swear by heaven, by earth, by Jerusalem) and collapses speech to two states: assertion or denial, 'anything beyond these is of evil.' That is exactly the bit: a variable over {0,1} with no admissible middle. In GF(2) every element is its own square (x^2 = x for x in {0,1}), the algebraic face of excluded-middle -- a yes that is fully yes, a no that is fully no, nothing in between to hedge with.
